title: vigna frutescens subsp. incana
intro: Vigna frutescens subsp. incana, also known as the hairy jack bean or hairy cowpea, is a plant species within the legume family that is native to tropical Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in digestion, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Vigna frutescens subsp. incana are often used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
